objective: Does the T17 class-weighted event-head loss fix (commit 5adaf396c, on main) let the model escape all-negative collapse and produce nonzero event detections?
manifest_reproduction_note: Byte-identical reproduction required staging all 40 candidate videos (28 jhong93 + 12 OpenTT), decode-verifying all 40, then EXCLUDING 9 specific videos (7 jhong93: usopen_2015_mens_final_federer_djokovic, usopen_2019_womens_osaka_gauff, usopen_2019_womens_round1_williams_sharapova, usopen_2020_womens_quarterfinal_osaka_rogers, usopen_2021_womens_semifinal_raducanu_sakkari, wimbledon_2019_womens_final_halep_williams, wimbledon_2019_womens_round1_gauff_williams; 2 OpenTT: game_4, test_2) to match the frozen corpus's own historical media_present pattern (jhong93 2603/3445 rows present, OpenTT 10/12 present) -- with all 40 present the build is functionally equivalent (same totals/rows/windows) but NOT byte-identical to the target SHA.
val_f1_trajectory_note: This is the training script's own internal validation metric (fixed tolerance_frames=2, apparently a ~0.5 decision threshold on positive-class probability -- TP only appears at steps where max_prob crosses ~0.5). It is non-monotonic and the FINAL checkpoint (step 16918) falls just below that internal threshold (max_prob 0.465), so tp=0/f1=0 there. This is NOT the preregistered gate; see step16918_eval below (threshold 0.05) for the actual answer, which is unambiguous.
preregistered_kill_applied: TP is clearly nonzero (44/70/107 across tolerances) AND max-probs clearly rising (mean 0.025 -> 0.137, ~5.5x; max 0.031 -> 0.357, ~11.6x) versus the step-9000 baseline on the identical frozen 50-clip public gate at the identical threshold 0.05. Kill condition (TP==0 AND max-probs flat) does NOT hold -> WEIGHTED_LOSS_WORKS per the recipe's own preregistered rule.
honest_caveats: Precision is very low at threshold 0.05 (FP >> TP; e.g. 466 FP vs 44 TP at tolerance 1) -- the fix escapes collapse, it does not yet produce a usable detector. No promotion is implied or claimed.

The internal training-validation trajectory is non-monotonic and the exact final checkpoint (step 16918) sits below that metric's own apparent ~0.5 threshold, so it alone would look like a null result; the discrepancy versus the threshold-0.05 gate is explained (different, much lower threshold) but is worth the next lane's attention if this checkpoint is used for anything beyond this yes/no question.

VM_RUN_PLAN.md has two stale/wrong sections found and worked around in this lane: (1) OpenTT section 3 assumes videos are embedded in the markup zip; the real video files are separate direct-fetch .mp4 URLs at the same lab.osai.ai path -- the zip contains only markup+segmentation masks. (2) unzip is not preinstalled on the pytorch-2-9-cu129-ubuntu-2204-nvidia-580 image, needed anyway once the URL was fixed to inspect the zip's real contents.

The checked-in scripts/fleet/lane_vm_startup.sh is a bare P0-1 scaffold with no shutdown rail or idle watchdog despite the dispatch instructions describing it as arming one; this lane worked around it with a locally-amended startup script (not committed) that prepends the VM_RUN_PLAN.md-mandated rail + watchdog.

One transient yt-dlp 403 on a single jhong93 video mid-batch aborted the first staging attempt (script used set -e with no per-video retry); fixed with a resumable retry script; the retry succeeded on its first attempt, so the 403 was transient, not a persistent block.
VERIFIED=0 remains binding; this is a bounded resume experiment per RETRAIN_RECIPE.md's own framing, not a promotion. No best_stack.json change.
teardown_evidence: gcloud compute instances delete pickleball-gpu-retrain --zone=us-central1-a exit 0; gcloud compute instances list --filter=labels.fable-lane=event_head_retrain returns empty; gcloud compute disks list has no pickleball-gpu-retrain disk; only pre-existing unrelated pickleball-a100-fleet1 (TERMINATED, untouched) remains under fable-fleet=pickleball.
artifacts_pulled: runs/lanes/event_head_retrain_20260720/vm_pull/ (eval_step9000_threshold_0.05.json, eval_step16918_threshold_0.05.json, train_lossfix/{last_event_head.pt,best_event_head.pt,train_manifest.json}, train_lossfix.log, eval_step9000.log, eval_step16918.log, manifest_a.json, LOCAL_SHA256SUMS.txt, PULL_SHA256SUMS_retrain.txt -- 9 artifacts, two-sided sha256 all match)
